sources.title: Delivery Hero reports adj. EBITDA up 30% in 2025, as Quick Commerce and key markets gain momentum
Uber Announces Results for First Quarter 2025
DoorDash Releases Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
Grab Reports Fourth Quarter and 2025 Results with First Full Year Net Profit
Instacart Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results

items.title: The category is moving from food delivery toward everyday local commerce
items.summary: Delivery Hero explicitly frames quick commerce and multi-vertical usage as part of its Everyday App strategy, while Uber Eats, DoorDash, Grab, Instacart, and regional platforms compete for grocery, convenience, and retail missions.
items.implication: Model restaurants, grocery, convenience, and retail in one local commerce domain rather than separating food delivery from quick commerce too early.

kpis: Monthly order frequency per active consumer

items.title: Fee clarity is a conversion problem, not only a policy problem
items.summary: Consumers compare convenience against total delivered cost. Delivery fee, service fee, tip, subscription benefit, promotion, and basket minimum mechanics all shape whether the order feels worth completing.
items.implication: Keep pricing, membership, basket, and checkout capabilities tightly linked, with fee clarity as a customer KPI and contribution margin as a business guardrail.

items.title: Delivery reliability is a three-sided system
items.summary: Consumer ETA, merchant prep readiness, courier assignment, route prediction, batching, and support recovery interact. Optimizing only one side can make the total marketplace worse.
items.implication: Treat order state, merchant prep, dispatch, routing, tracking, and issue recovery as one reliability system with shared metrics.

items.title: Merchant growth depends on operational fit, not just demand reach
items.summary: A merchant values incremental demand only if catalog maintenance, POS injection, courier timing, refunds, promotions, ads, and payout reconciliation do not create unmanaged operational cost.
items.implication: Model merchant onboarding, catalog, POS integration, prep workflow, promotions, ads, reporting, and settlement as a connected supply-side product.

items.title: Courier supply quality is driven by predictable earning time
items.summary: Courier retention depends on usable active time, clear offers, low merchant wait, rational batching, transparent pay, and fast issue response.
items.implication: Separate courier supply, dispatch optimization, earnings, payout, and safety ownership so courier economics are not hidden inside consumer delivery metrics.

items.title: Retail and grocery introduce harder catalog and substitution problems
items.summary: Grocery and retail delivery need SKU-level availability, weighted items, aisle or picker workflows, substitutions, and refund rules that are more complex than restaurant menus.
items.implication: Keep grocery and retail partner operations as a dedicated capability rather than treating them as a generic merchant catalog variant.

items.title: Retail media is becoming a meaningful marketplace monetization layer
items.summary: Large delivery marketplaces increasingly monetize merchant and brand demand through sponsored listings, promotions, and closed-loop attribution.
items.implication: Treat ads, promotions, attribution, merchant reporting, and experimentation as first-class product capabilities connected to search and conversion.

items.title: Marketplace risk spans all actors and money flows
items.summary: Refund abuse, promo abuse, fake accounts, courier collusion, merchant policy violations, restricted items, and payment fraud all affect trust and contribution margin.
items.implication: Keep identity, risk, ledger, support, and operations connected so trust decisions can use cross-actor evidence without weakening privacy and access controls.

items.title: Regional competition creates different operating constraints
items.summary: The market includes global players, super-apps, food-first regional leaders, and grocery-specific platforms. Labor rules, payment methods, city density, restaurant digitization, and consumer fee tolerance vary materially by region.
items.implication: Keep core product bricks global but allow pricing, courier work model, partner integrations, and compliance workflows to be market-configurable.

items.title: The most important product decisions need marketplace guardrails
items.summary: Ranking, pricing, batching, ads, promotions, and courier incentives can each improve one metric while damaging another side of the marketplace.
items.implication: Make experimentation, metric definitions, marketplace health, and guardrail monitoring part of the core operating model.
